articles.title: Jamie Dimon: JPMorgan could offer prediction market services to investors
articles.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon says the bank might start offering prediction market services to its investment clients. If America's largest bank enters the space, it would legitimize prediction markets for institutional investors and potentially funnel billions in new capital into political and economic forecasting.

articles.title: Prediction markets face DOJ scrutiny over well-timed bets on Nicolás Maduro capture, Iran strikes: sources
articles.summary: The Department of Justice is investigating suspiciously well-timed bets on prediction markets related to Nicolás Maduro's potential capture and Iranian military strikes, suggesting possible insider trading concerns. This scrutiny could trigger new regulatory crackdowns on prediction markets, particularly around geopolitical events where government officials might have advance knowledge.

articles.title: Most Americans Say Prediction Market Sports Betting Could Increase Harm
articles.summary: A new survey shows most Americans believe prediction market-style sports betting will cause more gambling harm than traditional wagering. This perception could fuel regulatory pushback against prediction markets just as they're gaining mainstream acceptance, potentially limiting their expansion beyond politics into sports and entertainment.

articles.title: ‘Astonishing and Appalling’: Senator Blasts MLB-Polymarket Deal
articles.summary: A senator is furious about MLB's partnership with Polymarket, likely viewing the deal as legitimizing gambling on sports through prediction markets. The outrage signals potential regulatory pushback that could threaten similar partnerships between traditional sports leagues and betting platforms.

articles.title: How polling failures, gambling legalization and political gridlock paved the way for the explosive rise of prediction markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets exploded into mainstream consciousness because traditional polling became unreliable, sports betting went legal nationwide, and Washington gridlock made political outcomes feel like pure coin flips. The convergence created perfect conditions for Americans to abandon trust in experts and start putting real money behind their political hunches instead.

articles.title: Prediction-market odds of a white-collar job wipeout have tripled since a bearish report went viral
articles.summary: A viral bearish report has spooked prediction markets, with odds of massive white-collar job losses jumping 3x from their previous levels. The swing shows how quickly market sentiment can shift when a single credible analysis hits the zeitgeist, creating potential arbitrage opportunities for contrarian bettors who think the panic is overblown.

articles.title: Prediction markets backlash builds possible stormcloud for 2027
articles.summary: Prediction markets are facing growing criticism and potential regulatory pushback that could culminate in serious restrictions by 2027. The backlash threatens to kill the industry's momentum just as it's gaining mainstream adoption, potentially forcing platforms offshore or underground.

articles.title: Inside Prediction Markets' Battle Royale: Kalshi Vs. Polymarket Vs. Congress/States
articles.summary: The prediction markets space is heating up as Kalshi and Polymarket face off while navigating a maze of congressional scrutiny and state-level regulations. This regulatory battle will determine which platforms survive, what markets Americans can actually bet on, and whether prediction markets become mainstream or get strangled in red tape.

articles.title: Watch Prediction Markets Shouldn't Include Everything: Smarkets
articles.summary: Smarkets is arguing that prediction markets work best when they stick to clearly defined, verifiable events rather than expanding into every possible topic under the sun. This matters because overreach into subjective or unmeasurable outcomes could undermine the credibility and accuracy that makes prediction markets valuable in the first place.

articles.title: Feds Sniffing Around Polymarket After Suspicious Bets
articles.summary: Federal investigators are examining unusual betting patterns on Polymarket that suggest possible market manipulation or insider trading. This probe could trigger the first major regulatory crackdown on prediction markets, potentially reshaping how these platforms operate in the US.

articles.title: At least three more Air Force members questioned on Iran war Polymarket betting
articles.summary: The Air Force is investigating at least three more service members for betting on Iran war markets on Polymarket, expanding beyond earlier cases. Military personnel having inside information on conflict decisions creates serious market integrity issues that could prompt regulators to restrict defense-related betting markets.

articles.title: NFL asks prediction market operators to refrain from 'objectionable bets'
articles.summary: The NFL is pushing prediction market operators to avoid offering bets on scenarios that could damage the league's reputation or encourage problematic behavior. This signals growing tension between sports leagues trying to protect their brand and the prediction market industry's push to offer comprehensive betting options on everything.
